source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager, API Platform
source_jobs.description:
WHO IS AIWYN AND WHAT DO WE DO?

Aiwyn is transforming the way accounting firms manage the entire revenue lifecycle. Backed by top-tier investors like Bessemer, KKR, and Revolution, we’re one of the fastest-growing scale-up SaaS companies in the world. We build category-defining technology—and we’re doing it with world-class people, processes, and products.

To learn more, visit our website https://aiwyn.ai/




THE ROLE

We're looking for a Senior Product Manager to own our platform integration and API roadmap.

Aiwyn is part of an ecosystem of software solutions that accounting firms may use to operate their business. Many of these systems provide complimentary functionality to Aiwyn’s core business, or are foundational systems of record. To be the platform for modern accounting firms, we must provide seamless integration with the tools and systems that matter to our customers.

This is a high-leverage, high impact role that is critical to our success as we scale rapidly. You'll work closely with engineering, design, customer success, and go-to-market teams. Your work will touch virtually every part of the product, and every customer we serve.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the integration roadmap. Identify which systems matter most to firms, prioritize ruthlessly, and ship integrations that customers actually adopt.

 - Define our platform and API strategy. Decide what we expose, to whom, and how. Shape the long-term vision for Aiwyn as the connective tissue of a modern accounting firm's tech stack.

 - Go deep with customers and partners. Sit with firms to understand their workflows across systems, and build direct relationships with the vendors whose products they rely on every day.

 - Partner cross-functionally to execute. Work hand-in-hand with engineering and design to ship, and with sales, CS, and marketing to bring to market.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Must haves:

 - 5–8 years of product management experience

 - Excellent product roadmapping: you can figure out which problems are worth solving, prioritize ruthlessly, and communicate your thinking with clarity

 - Strong product lifecycle instincts: you go deep on customer problems and create solutions that actually fit

 - Execution chops: you know how to ship software, keep teams aligned, and course-correct when things go sideways

 - An incredible trajectory: you're becoming a domain expert in everything you touch, and you bring clarity and energy to every room you're in

Nice-to-haves (not required):

 - Experience with platforms

 - Experience owning API products

 - Startup experience- you're comfortable with ambiguity and a fast pace




WHY WORK AT AIWYN?

 - The big-picture value proposition of this role is simple: join us, and you'll be paid competitively to have freedom in solving worthwhile, challenging problems alongside other A-players at a fast-growing SaaS startup led by an experienced and successful team with industry experience

 - AI-by-Default - you'll have access to best-in-class AI tools and work in an environment that embraces an AI-by-default mindset, where curiosity and experimentation are part of how we operate

 - Remote-first organization

 - Competitive compensation and benefits

 - Stock options

 - 401(k) matching

 - Adventure Travel Stipend on each anniversary of your employment with Aiwyn


Our Values:

 - Relentless by Design

 - Owners not Renters

 - Trust at the Core



Aiwyn is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran status, race, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable laws, regulations and ordinances.

source_jobs.title: Delivery Lead
source_jobs.description:
About Aiwyn

Who is Aiwyn and what do we do?

Aiwyn is building the operating layer for accounting firms — automating the workflows that sit between client engagement and getting paid. Our platform spans Payments, Engagements, and Practice Management, and we're deploying it inside some of the most respected accounting firms in the country, including Big 4 and Top 25 firms.




ABOUT THE ROLE


The Delivery Lead is the Directly Responsible Individual for Aiwyn's most complex, high-stakes customer implementations. Where an Implementation Specialist owns a single-firm, defined-scope deployment, the IPM owns programs — rolling M&A playbooks, multi-phase enterprise rollouts, and multi-product deployments that span quarters and involve multiple internal teams and firm-side stakeholders simultaneously.


This role sits at the center of Aiwyn's Advanced Implementation delivery model, leading a cross-functional trio alongside a Solutions Architect and Implementation Engineer. The IPM is accountable for the overall success of the program — scope, timeline, delivery quality, and customer confidence — while coordinating execution across every workstream.


This is not a coordination role. It's an ownership role. The success or failure of the program lands here.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN


Program Ownership & Delivery Leadership Own the end-to-end success of complex, multi-product customer implementations. Develop and maintain comprehensive program plans that coordinate multiple workstreams, phases, and product deployments. Ensure all implementation activity stays on schedule, on scope, and aligned to delivery objectives. When something threatens the program, you see it first and act on it.


Cross-Functional Leadership Lead the Advanced Implementation Delivery Trio — Solutions Architect and Implementation Engineer — driving alignment and accountability across each function without direct authority over the success of the project. Coordinate across Implementation, Product, Engineering, Customer Success, and Support to ensure nothing falls through the cracks between teams.


Customer & Executive Engagement Serve as the primary delivery leader for enterprise customers throughout the implementation. Facilitate executive-level conversations on program progress, key decisions, and delivery strategy. Manage escalations with clarity and composure. Maintain customer confidence even when the program is under pressure.


Scope & Risk Management Own scope integrity across the program — identify scope creep early, manage change control, and communicate tradeoffs to both the customer and internal leadership. Maintain a live risk register. Surface risks before they become fires and drive resolution with urgency.


Solution Coordination Partner closely with the Solutions Architect to ensure the proposed solution is being executed as designed. Ensure the Implementation Engineer has what they need to execute technical workstreams on time. Identify misalignment between design and execution early and drive it to resolution.


Workflow Solutioning & Product Expertise


Serve as a product expert and trusted advisor to the customer throughout the implementation — not just a project coordinator. When a customer's existing workflows need to change to get the most out of Aiwyn, you lead that conversation. You understand the product deeply enough to guide firms on how it should be configured and used, translate their operational requirements into the right implementation approach, and push back constructively when a requested workflow runs counter to how the product is designed to work. Customers should leave every session with you having a clearer picture of both what they purchased and how to make it work for their firm.


Ongoing Program Cadence Establish and own the operational rhythm of the program — weekly syncs, milestone reviews, status reporting, and executive readouts. Keep all stakeholders informed without creating meeting overhead that slows the team down.

source_jobs.title: Senior Accountant
source_jobs.description:
Who is Aiwyn and what do we do?

Aiwyn is the first complete platform for modern accounting firms. Backed by top-tier investors like Bessemer, KKR, and Revolution, we’re one of the fastest-growing scale-up SaaS companies in the world. We build category-defining technology—and we’re doing it with world-class people, processes, and products.

 

To learn more, visit our website https://aiwyn.ai/

 

About the Role

Aiwyn is a PE-backed (KKR) SaaS and payments company that has completed four acquisitions in the past two years. We're building the finance infrastructure to match that growth, and we're looking for a Senior Accountant who wants to be part of that build, not just maintain what's already there.


This role reports to the VP of Accounting and works closely with the Assistant Controller & Accounting Manager across a multi-entity, multi-revenue-stream business. You'll own meaningful areas of the month-end close, serve as a first line of quality review, and operate with the kind of independence that comes from real experience. Our environment is complex — consolidated reporting across multiple subsidiaries, usage-based billing, SaaS subscriptions, and PE-level reporting standards. We’re looking for someone who is excited to take on this challenge and grow through a new learning experience.


We're a small, high-performing team that values Relentlessness, Ownership, and Trust. Some of the processes you'll work with are well-established; others you'll help build from scratch. If you're the kind of person who sees a gap and fills it without being asked, who'd rather solve the root cause than patch a workaround, and who gets genuinely excited about using technology and automation to make things work better, this is the right environment for you.


Month-end close and audit season bring hard deadlines, and we meet them with professionalism and composure. In return, you'll work alongside people who hold themselves to the same standard they ask of you, with direct visibility into how the business operates, real ownership of your work, and room to grow as the team and company scale.



Key Responsibilities:

 - Own month-end close execution for assigned areas, including balance sheet account reconciliations (Deferred and Unbilled Revenue, Capitalized Software, Capitalized Commissions, Internal Use Software) and bank reconciliations across all entities, including FBO accounts.

 - Serve as a first line of review on close deliverables, identifying anomalies, researching variances, and escalating issues intelligently before they reach the Assistant Controller or Accounting Manager.

 - Oversee deal setup and billing processes to ensure accuracy, completeness, and alignment with contractual terms.

 - Support revenue recognition and billing reconciliation workflows across multiple revenue streams.

 - Partner with the Assistant Controller and Accounting Manager to facilitate the annual audit, including preparing documentation, and ensuring timely resolution of outstanding items.

 - Develop and continuously improve financial reporting procedures, identifying opportunities for automation and process optimization.

 - Ensure financial statements are prepared in accordance with GAAP and audit readiness requirements are consistently met.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with internal stakeholders to support business initiatives and enhance the accuracy and efficiency of financial reporting.

 - Execute ad hoc analysis, projects, and reporting requests as assigned by leadership.
   
   

Qualifications:

 - Bachelor's degree in Accounting

 - CPA or CPA candidate preferred

 - 4+ years of progressive accounting experience; a combination of public and private accounting experience strongly preferred, with at least 2 years in public accounting

 - Experience in a SaaS, payments, or multi-entity environment preferred

 - Proficiency in NetSuite or comparable enterprise ERP

 - Strong understanding of GAAP and regulatory requirements

 - Dem

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager, Foundations and Reporting
source_jobs.description:
WHO IS AIWYN AND WHAT DO WE DO?

Aiwyn is transforming the way accounting firms manage the entire revenue lifecycle. Backed by top-tier investors like Bessemer, KKR, and Revolution, we’re one of the fastest-growing scale-up SaaS companies in the world. We build category-defining technology—and we’re doing it with world-class people, processes, and products.

To learn more, visit our website https://aiwyn.ai/




THE ROLE

We're looking for a Senior Product Manager to own two critical areas of our platform: Foundations and Reporting.

The Foundations team owns the core components and systems that power the entire Aiwyn platform. Getting this right means every feature feels cohesive and every customer experiences a product that just works.

The Reporting product gives accounting firms the visibility they need to run their businesses. You'll shape the analytics experience our customers depend on to make decisions.

This is a high-leverage, high impact role that is critical to our success as we scale rapidly. You'll work closely with engineering, design, customer success, and go-to-market teams. Your work will touch virtually every part of the product, and every customer we serve.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the roadmap for Aiwyn's foundational platform layer - the shared components, systems, and patterns that make our platform work

 - Define strategy for shared systems

 - Own the strategy and roadmap for Aiwyn's customer-facing analytics and reporting product

 - Develop a deep understanding of how accounting firms use data to manage their practices

 - Work with customers, customer success, and sales to identify reporting gaps that create the most friction and unlock the most value

 - Define the experience for how users discover, interact with, save, and share reports

WHY WORK AT AIWYN?

 - AI-by-Default - you'll have access to best-in-class AI tools and work in an environment that embraces an AI-by-default mindset, where curiosity and experimentation are part of how we operate

 - Stock options

 - 401(k) matching

 - Adventure Travel Stipend on each anniversary of your employment with Aiwyn


Our Values:

 - Relentless by Design

 - Owners not Renters

 - Trust at the Core



Aiwyn is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran status, race, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable laws, regulations and ordinances.



Candidate information will be treated in accordance with our CCPA privacy notice which can be found here: https://www.aiwyn.ai/ccp

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, AI Enablement
source_jobs.description:
WHO IS AIWYN AND WHAT DO WE DO?

Aiwyn is the first complete platform for modern Accounting Firms. Backed by top-tier investors like Bessemer, KKR, and Revolution, we're one of the fastest-growing scale-up SaaS companies in the world. We build category-defining technology, and we're doing it with world-class people, processes, and products.

To learn more, visit our website https://aiwyn.ai/

We work with the majority of the Top 500 firms in the country. We're modernizing the software accounting firms rely on every day, replacing decades-old systems and brittle integrations with tools they can actually trust, and we have rare product-market fit in an industry that's about to change permanently.

We're hiring a Software Engineer for AI Enablement on the Productivity team. The job is to make AI the default way work happens at Aiwyn, for every team including engineering itself.



THE ROLE

AI only compounds when it's woven into how an organization actually works, not bolted on as another tool. We need a systems thinker who will work alongside teams across the company (sales, success, ops, finance, and engineering), see how work actually gets done, and remove the friction. The toolkit is broad and the right answer depends on the problem.

You'll own the AI adoption layer that sits across the whole org, with engineering as a first-class user. Making engineers measurably faster with AI tooling is one of the highest-leverage applications of this role, and it's where you'll often start. The mandate is broad: make AI the default way work happens here, with whatever combination of tools, agents, and systems that takes.



WHAT GOOD LOOKS LIKE

 - Our internal AI assistant is the first stop, not the last resort. People use it because it works. Information flows are mapped, gaps get followed up on, and answer quality improves week over week.

 - Every team has at least one custom agent that earns its keep. You've sat with the team, watched them work, and shipped something narrow that takes real toil off their plate.

 - Engineers feel measurably faster. Agent-augmented engineering (Claude Code, Cursor, agent code review, agent QA) is the default workflow, not an experiment. PR cycle time and time-to-first-deploy for new hires move in the right direction.

 - Documentation is alive. It updates when the code changes. When it's wrong, an agent flags it. When somebody asks a question the docs should have answered, the docs get better.

 - Adoption is visible. We can see who's using what, where the holdouts are, and what's actually moving the metric. The "I'm bought in" claim gets backed by data.

 - You're the person other engineers come to when they want to bring AI into a workflow. You've built the foundation that lets them do it themselves.



DAY-TO-DAY

 - Sit with users. You'll spend hours in the workflows of sales, success, ops, finance, and engineering. Not because you have to. Because that's where the problems are.

 - Ship narrow agents. Tight scope, real users, fast iteration. Whatever combination of agents, integrations, and internal tools the situation calls for.

 - Operate our internal AI assistant as a product. Triage what's working, fill the gaps, follow up on questions that didn't get good answers, push the loop until quality is consistently high.

 - Raise the engineering productivity bar. Agent-driven testing, review, and verification as part of the everyday loop. Automated migration tooling that compresses weeks into hours.

 - Build the patterns and primitives that let other engineers do this kind of work themselves: skills, agent templates, MCP servers, eval harnesses.

 - Measure adoption honestly. Build the dashboards that tell us who's actually using AI, where, and what the leverage is. Use them to make the next set of bets.

 - Drive adoption. Some of the work is technical, some is human. You can sit with someone, watch them try a tool, figure out whether the gap is in the workflow or in the tool itself, and close

source_jobs.title: Solutions Architect
source_jobs.description:
WHO IS AIWYN AND WHAT DO WE DO?


Aiwyn is the first complete platform for modern accounting firms. We unify practice management, payments, client experience, and tax in a single system built for scale. Backed by top-tier investors like Bessemer, KKR, and Revolution, we're one of the fastest-growing scale-up SaaS companies in the world, trusted by the nation's Top 500 CPA firms. We build category-defining technology and we're doing it with world-class people, processes, and products.




To learn more, visit our website: www.Aiwyn.ai http://www.Aiwyn.ai





WHO ARE YOU?

You are often the most technically credible person in the room. You lead with curiosity, seek to understand, and influence outcomes through clarity, judgment, and trust.




You are equally comfortable whiteboarding an integration with a developer, pressure-testing feasibility with Product and Engineering, or presenting a clear technical architecture to a firm’s Executive Team.




As a Solutions Architect at Aiwyn, you serve as the bridge between what a firm needs, what our platform can support, and what our Implementation team can successfully execute.




This is a build role. You'll join the founding class of a brand-new Solutions Architecture function: defining the playbooks, scoping standards, and discovery frameworks that will scale with us.




There's no manual yet. You'll write it. If that energizes you rather than intimidates you, keep reading.




We are AI-first operators. You use AI tools by default: discovery prep, scoping documentation, and accelerating your own product learning. And you can credibly represent that mindset to firms adopting AI and agents in their own workflows.





KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own the technical evaluation end-to-end. Lead technical discovery, feasibility assessment, and solution architecture across the pre-sale cycle. You're the definitive answer to "can we do this, and what will it take?"

 - Design systems-level solutions. Translate firm workflow requirements into integration blueprints spanning APIs, webhooks, data migration, ETL, and SSO — and assess feasibility without pulling in Engineering for every question.

 - Produce contract-quality scoping documents that Delivery Leads can execute without re-discovery. Your work product sets the implementation up to succeed.

 - Run executive rooms. Build credibility with firm-side CTOs, COOs, and partners and shape deal strategy alongside our AEs, influencing evaluation criteria and accelerating technical buyer trust.

 - Uncover what firms actually need including what they can't articulate and synthesize findings into designs, timelines, and constraints. Knowing when to say no is part of the job.

 - Build the playbook. Turn ad hoc wins into a reproducible structure that reduces ambiguity for everyone around you.

 - Carry field signal back to Product with specificity patterns across engagements, not one-off feature requests.

 - Show up in person. On-sites and joint sales calls are a material part of this role, and you genuinely enjoy them.
   


QUALIFICATIONS

 - Proven pre-sales, post-sales, solutions architecture, or solutions engineering experience in B2B SaaS, ideally FinTech or accounting technology, at a fast-growth 0-1 company or an early-series growth-stage company.

 - Ideally have fluency in the accounting tech stack: GL systems, practice management platforms, document management, and billing infrastructure with an understanding of how Big 4, Top 25, and regional firm environments differ.

 - Command of APIs, webhooks, data migration, ETL, and SSO sufficient to design integrations and judge feasibility independently.

 - Hands-on fluency with AI tools and AI-assisted workflows in production and the ability to speak credibly to where the technology is headed.

 - Exceptional communication across audiences: technical depth at the whiteboard, executive presence in the boardroom.

 - Coachability and self-awareness: you can walk us through a deal you scope

source_jobs.title: Director of Engineering
source_jobs.description:
WHO IS AIWYN AND WHAT DO WE DO?

Aiwyn is the first complete platform for modern Accounting Firms. Backed by top-tier investors like Bessemer, KKR, and Revolution, we're one of the fastest-growing scale-up SaaS companies in the world. We build category-defining technology, and we're doing it with world-class people, processes, and products.

To learn more, visit our website https://www.aiwyn.ai/.

We work with the majority of the Top 500 firms in the country. We're replacing 40-year-old tax engines and brittle integrations with software accounting firms can actually trust, and we have rare product-market fit in an industry that's about to change permanently. We're hiring a Director of Platform Engineering to lead the foundation it all runs on.



THE ROLE

Platform owns the parts of Aiwyn that everything else is built on: our build and cloud infrastructure, our core IP data ingestion and normalization pipeline, our tax engine, our implementations engineering, and the productivity group elevating how the whole company works in the AI era. You'll lead five teams and roughly thirty engineers today, with engineering managers on each team and a peer product leader you'll partner with closely. Headcount will grow.

This is a platform role with a lot of product work in it. The ingestion and normalization pipeline and our tax engine are core IP, and the work to make them best in class is going to come straight from your org. You won't just be enabling other people's products. You'll be building some of the most important ones at Aiwyn.



WHAT THE PLATFORM DELIVERS

When this role is going well, the rest of Aiwyn feels it. Specifically:

 - Product teams ship on top of platform without thinking about it. Our internal APIs, primitives, and golden paths are well defined enough that other engineers reach for them by default. They don't fight infrastructure, they don't reinvent integrations, and they don't get blocked waiting on us.

 - Our ingestion pipeline and tax engine are best in class. Firms trust us with the data and calculations their entire practice depends on. The platform team is delivering accuracy, performance, and breadth that legacy providers can't match.

 - Implementations land cleanly. New customers get to value fast. Engineering, product, and the customer team have a shared view of what's happening, and the same bug doesn't surface twice.

 - Production is boring. Deploys are daily, incidents get diagnosed at the root, and the team takes infrastructure, change management, observability, and on-call seriously enough that surprises are rare.

 - The whole company moves faster because of how this team works. The productivity team is shipping leverage that compounds across engineering, product, and operations. AI isn't a side initiative, it's the default way work happens.

 - The team is legible. Anyone in the company can see what platform is working on, why, and how it's going.


DAY TO DAY

 - Lead the leaders. Coach and develop EMs across infrastructure, foundations, tax engine, implementations, and productivity. Hire when the bar requires it, performance manage when it doesn't, and build a leadership team that can scale.

 - Own the operating model. Define the rhythms, metrics, and standards that make the org legible and accountable, including cycle planning, change management, design review, incident response, and on-call quality.

 - Set technical direction. Partner with your engineers, EMs, and anyone else with strong opinions on the multi-year evolution of the tax engine, the ingestion and normalization pipeline, and our core infrastructure on GCP.

 - Hold the bar on operational excellence. Get us to daily deploys. Push incident response toward root cause. Build the metrics and forums that make execution legible and force the right conversations.

 - Partner across the company. Work shoulder to shoulder with your peer product leader on platform, with the product engineering org, with support, and with GTM. Earn tr

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager, Payments
source_jobs.description:
WHO IS AIWYN AND WHAT DO WE DO?

Aiwyn is transforming the way accounting firms manage the entire revenue lifecycle. Backed by top-tier investors like Bessemer, KKR, and Revolution, we’re one of the fastest-growing scale-up SaaS companies in the world. We build category-defining technology—and we’re doing it with world-class people, processes, and products.

To learn more, visit our website https://aiwyn.ai/

 


THE ROLE

We're looking for a Senior Product Manager to own Payments, Aiwyn's largest product and the main revenue driver of the business.

This is a GM-level seat for Aiwyn’s flagship product. You'll own the strategy, roadmap, P&L, customer relationships, and the banking partnerships that power money movement. You'll partner with a dedicated execution-focused PM who owns day-to-day delivery, which frees you to lead at the strategy and ownership level. You'll work closely with engineering, design, customer success, and go-to-market, and your decisions will shape the trajectory of the business.

 


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own Payments strategy and the P&L. Set the direction for Aiwyn's flagship product and own the results.

 - Own the roadmap. Decide what we build and in what order, prioritize ruthlessly, and ship what firms actually adopt.

 - Own our banking and payments partnerships. Build and manage the relationships that power processing and money movement.

 - Go deep with customers. Sit with firms, own the most important accounts, and turn what you learn into product wins and retention.

 - Partner cross-functionally to execute. Work with a dedicated execution PM, engineering, and design to ship, and with sales, CS, and marketing to bring it to market.

 


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Must haves:

 - 5–8 years of product management experience

 - Payments, fintech, or money-movement experience: you understand processing, billing, or banking partnerships well enough to make strategic calls without a ramp

 - You've owned a product as a business: strategy, roadmap, and a revenue or P&L outcome you can point to

 - Excellent product roadmapping: you can figure out which problems are worth solving, prioritize ruthlessly, and communicate your thinking with clarity

 - Execution chops: you know how to ship software, keep teams aligned, and course-correct when things go sideways

 - An incredible trajectory: you're becoming a domain expert in everything you touch, and you bring clarity and energy to every room you're in

Nice-to-haves (not required):

 - Vertical SaaS or accounting/CPA industry experience

 - Experience owning sponsor-bank or banking partnerships

 - Experience running a large product migration or platform consolidation

 - Startup experience- you're comfortable with ambiguity and a fast pace

 


WHY WORK AT AIWYN?

 - The big-picture value proposition of this role is simple: join us, and you'll be paid competitively to have freedom in solving worthwhile, challenging problems alongside other A-players at a fast-growing SaaS startup led by an experienced and successful team with industry experience

 - AI-by-Default - you'll have access to best-in-class AI tools and work in an environment that embraces an AI-by-default mindset, where curiosity and experimentation are part of how we operate

 - Remote-first organization

 - Competitive compensation and benefits

 - Stock options

 - 401(k) matching

 - Adventure Travel Stipend on each anniversary of your employment with Aiwyn


Our Values:

 - Relentless by Design

 - Owners not Renters

 - Trust at the Core

 

Aiwyn is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran status, race, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic prote

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Practice
source_jobs.description:
WHO IS AIWYN AND WHAT DO WE DO?

Aiwyn is the first complete platform for modern Accounting Firms. Backed by top-tier investors like Bessemer, KKR, and Revolution, we're one of the fastest-growing scale-up SaaS companies in the world. We build category-defining technology, and we're doing it with world-class people, processes, and products.

To learn more, visit our website https://aiwyn.ai/

We work with the majority of the Top 500 firms in the country. We're replacing brittle, decades-old accounting practice management systems with software accounting firms can actually trust, and we have rare product-market fit in an industry that's about to change permanently. We're hiring a senior software engineer to join the team that owns our accounting practice management platform: the system of record for how firms track the work, bill for it, and collect, end to end.



THE ROLE

Our accounting practice management platform is one of the most strategic pieces of the Aiwyn platform — where the work firms do turns into revenue tracked, billed, and collected. Aiwyn Practice is the heart of it. It's the foundation we're building out so firms can manage the full lifecycle: project and time tracking, resource management, billing, invoicing, and accounts receivable, with the visibility that keeps the firm confident every piece of work is tracked, billed, and accounted for.

You'll be a senior engineer on the team behind this work, partnering closely with a deeply tenured group of accounting and firm operations experts who set the bar for what "correct" means.



DAY-TO-DAY

 - Build the systems firms run their business on. Own the services, infrastructure, and confidence metrics that let practice management grow at speed without sacrificing accuracy or trust. Make the iteration loop faster every cycle.

 - Work in the trenches with the domain experts. They've been doing this for decades. They're your closest collaborators and your toughest users. Spend real time with them, understand how accounting firms run and translate that depth into the systems you build.

 - Build and extend the APIs that Aiwyn, firms, and partners build on top of. Integrations are the wedge. Make connecting firm systems, data sources, and downstream tools seamless, and the surface area something engineers actually want to use.

 - Take projects from spec to ship to scale. Own the work end to end, including the parts outside your comfort zone.



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years of professional software engineering experience, with a track record of taking full ownership of the projects you take on.

 - Hands-on technical depth in domains where correctness matters. You've shipped real systems where edge cases, accuracy, and domain depth were non-negotiable.

 - Experience in project management and/or fintech software. This is the domain you'd be building in, so experience here is what we're looking for — though it's not a hard requirement. You understand why getting these systems right is non-negotiable.

 - Demonstrated ability to bridge engineering and a non-engineering domain. Engineers and finance/accounting experts (or doctors, or lawyers, or any deep domain expert) think wildly differently. You've worked closely with non-engineering experts before, taken their judgment seriously, and built shared language and shared artifacts that let both sides do their best work. We've watched smart people struggle here, and this is the part of the job we look at hardest.

 - AI-native fluency. AI tooling is woven into how you work and ship, and you have a clear point of view on where it helps and where it creates noise.

 - Comfortable across the stack. Backend, frontend, or infrastructure — you can reason about whatever the problem actually requires.

 - Bonus: experience building or contributing to a billing, ledger, workflow, role based access control (RBAC), authentication, or comparable system. Practice management, billing, pricing, underwriting, claims adjudication,

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Payments
source_jobs.description:
WHO IS AIWYN AND WHAT DO WE DO?

Aiwyn is the first complete platform for modern Accounting Firms. Backed by top-tier investors like Bessemer, KKR, and Revolution, we're one of the fastest-growing scale-up SaaS companies in the world. We build category-defining technology, and we're doing it with world-class people, processes, and products.

To learn more, visit our website https://aiwyn.ai/

We work with the majority of the Top 500 firms in the country. We're replacing brittle, decades-old payments and collections workflows with software accounting firms can actually trust, and we have rare product-market fit in an industry that's about to change permanently. We're hiring a senior software engineer to join the team that owns our payments platform: the system that moves money between firms and their clients, already processing payments at scale and now the backbone for how modern firms get paid end to end.



THE ROLE

Payments is one of the most strategic pieces of the Aiwyn platform — the place where the work firms do turns into revenue collected. It's the foundation we're building out so firms can manage the full payment lifecycle: invoicing, collections, payment methods, schedules and installments, reconciliation, and the visibility that keeps both firms and their clients confident that every dollar is accounted for.

You'll be a senior engineer on the team behind this work, partnering closely with a deeply tenured group of accounting and payment experts who set the bar for what "correct" means.



DAY-TO-DAY

 - Build the systems that move money reliably. Own the services, infrastructure, and confidence metrics that let payments grow at speed without sacrificing accuracy or trust. Make the iteration loop faster every cycle.

 - Work in the trenches with the domain experts. They've been doing this for decades. They're your closest collaborators and your toughest users. Spend real time with them, understand what it actually takes to invoice, collect, and reconcile across firms and jurisdictions, and translate that depth into the systems you build.

 - Build and extend the APIs that Aiwyn, firms, and partners build on top of. Integrations are the wedge. Make connecting payment processors, banks, and firm systems seamless, and the surface area something engineers actually want to use.

 - Take projects from spec to ship to scale. Own the work end to end, including the parts outside your comfort zone.



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years of professional software engineering experience, with a track record of taking full ownership of the projects you take on.

 - Hands-on technical depth in domains where correctness matters. You've shipped real systems where edge cases, accuracy, and domain depth were non-negotiable.

 - Experience with fintech and payments. Direct work on payment systems, money movement, or collections. You understand why getting money movement right is non-negotiable, and you've shipped systems where that showed.

 - Demonstrated ability to bridge engineering and a non-engineering domain. Engineers and finance/accounting experts (or doctors, or lawyers, or any deep domain expert) think wildly differently. You've worked closely with non-engineering experts before, taken their judgment seriously, and built shared language and shared artifacts that let both sides do their best work. We've watched smart people struggle here, and this is the part of the job we look at hardest.

 - AI-native fluency. AI tooling is woven into how you work and ship, and you have a clear point of view on where it helps and where it creates noise.

 - Comfortable across the stack. Backend, frontend, or infrastructure — you can reason about whatever the problem actually requires.

 - Bonus: deeper fintech and/or payments experience. Building or contributing to a payments, billing, ledger, pricing, underwriting, claims adjudication, compliance, or benefits system — anywhere correctness is non-negotiable and the system has to compose hu

notes: Aiwyn is a fast-growing SaaS company in the accounting space, with a strong focus on product development and engineering.
